principles.label: Tech Responsibility & Engineered Kindness
principles.summary: Tech creators are inherently responsible for the consequences of their platforms and must engineer strict behavioral guardrails to promote kindness.
principles.details: The internet was built without guardrails, allowing toxic offline behaviors to thrive. Just as bad behavior can go viral, platforms can be intentionally designed with constraints that promote and amplify positive, kind interactions. Leaders must enforce a zero-tolerance policy for hate speech and harassment.
principles.representative_quote: The minute you encourage someone to use a piece of technology, you are inherently responsible.

principles.label: Self-Worth Does Not Equal Net Worth
principles.summary: Achieving societal wealth or status doesn't guarantee emotional or spiritual fulfillment; true success is entirely internal.
principles.details: You can reach the absolute pinnacle of external success, such as taking a company public and becoming a billionaire, and still feel completely empty if you lack inner peace. The measuring stick for success must be consciously chosen rather than dictated by external forces.
principles.representative_quote: I will tell you that on paper at my on paper wealthiest according to society standards I was spiritually the poorest I've ever been.

principles.label: Turn Personal Pain into Collective Strength
principles.summary: Moving your mindset from 'me' to 'we' gives you the perseverance to build something meaningful and endure the hardships of entrepreneurship.
principles.details: Instead of seeking revenge after a traumatic event or setback, ask how your experience can help others. When you are serving the greater good of humanity, the purpose fuels you to keep going even when you are exhausted.
principles.representative_quote: I'm going to take what almost killed me and I'm going to make us stronger.

principles.label: Focus on Core Inputs, Not Outputs
principles.summary: Evaluate business health by looking at foundational drivers (inputs) rather than lagging metrics (outputs) like revenue or sheer user volume.
principles.details: Chasing outputs like revenue or sheer volume of registrations leads to short-term growth that is eventually lost because the core user experience degrades. Focusing on whether members are actually finding what they came for ensures sustainable success.
principles.representative_quote: A good business does not focus on outputs. A good business focuses on the core inputs that matter most.

principles.label: Empower Women to Make the First Move
principles.summary: Modernize outdated relationship dynamics by empowering women to initiate contact, leveling the playing field in both dating and business.
principles.details: Despite progress in the workplace, dating dynamics remained outdated, leaving women disempowered and waiting for men to initiate. Flipping this dynamic builds confidence for women and relieves men of the exhausting pressure to always make the first move.
principles.representative_quote: Why is there this standard that, as a woman, you can get your dream job but you can't talk to a guy first? Let's make dating feel more modern.

principles.label: Technology Should Facilitate Offline Connection
principles.summary: The ultimate goal of connection technology is to get people off their phones and into real-world interactions.
principles.details: Success for a dating or friendship app is not maximizing screen time. If a user spends hours a day on a dating app like they do on a content feed, the app is failing its core purpose of combating loneliness.
principles.representative_quote: I do not want you to stare at your phone all day. I fail my job if I trap you on the phone.

principles.label: Opposites Attract, But Opposite Values Don't
principles.summary: While lifestyle choices and personality quirks can differ wildly between partners, a relationship cannot survive if core foundational values do not align.
principles.details: You can have different habits (e.g., waking up early vs. late, liking sweets vs. hating them), but fundamental alignment on values is non-negotiable for long-term compatibility.
principles.representative_quote: I think opposites attract but opposite values don't attract. I'm a firm believer of this.

principles.label: Trust Your Intuition Over Playbooks
principles.summary: Listen to and trust your intuition over societal expectations or traditional business playbooks.
principles.details: You don't need to read traditional business books to succeed if you have deep conviction in your mission and intense loyalty to your customer. Intuition helps you discern what is right for you, even when society points you elsewhere.
principles.representative_quote: I am here because I listened to my intuition. I trusted myself and I knew the difference between what was right for me and what was right for somebody else.

principles.label: True Feminism Means Equality for Everyone
principles.summary: Feminism is about equality, compassion, and lifting each other up, which includes building true partnerships with men.
principles.details: Alienating 'the good guys' goes against the core of feminism. Men can be great mentors, and releasing everyone from polarized gender roles benefits both men and women.
principles.representative_quote: It’s not really living up to true feminism, which is really equality for everyone, right?

principles.label: Evolve by Leaning Into Customer Stress
principles.summary: Evolve your product by listening to what exhausts your customers, even if it means changing a core founding feature.
principles.details: Founders make the first move by launching the core product, but after that, they should closely observe how users are adapting the product and build features to remove friction and support emergent behaviors.
principles.representative_quote: The users are what give us every move we make, except for the very first move.

principles.label: AI as an Emotionally Intelligent Matchmaker
principles.summary: AI can understand our relationship needs better than we can articulate them ourselves.
principles.details: Because humans struggle to identify their true relationship needs and often filter out compatible partners based on rigid criteria, an emotionally intelligent AI can step in to make better matchmaking choices and act as a dating concierge.
principles.representative_quote: We can’t articulate what we need in a relationship.

frameworks.label: Emotional Acceptance Process
frameworks.summary: A method for processing hurt feelings, rejection, or the pain of others' opinions without letting it consume you.
frameworks.details: 1. Notice the hurt feelings without shaming yourself for having them. 2. Look directly at the feeling and acknowledge it objectively. 3. Identify the feeling as a wounded part of yourself. 4. Accept these pieces so the negative emotion doesn't take over your life.
frameworks.representative_quote: I don't shame myself for having hurt feelings... I just kind of look at it and I stare at it and say, 'Okay, that's okay. That's like a wounded part of you...'

frameworks.label: The 'Why' Test for Business
frameworks.summary: A diagnostic to ensure a business or initiative is built on a sustainable, purpose-driven foundation.
frameworks.details: When starting a business or facing severe setbacks, ask yourself: 'Who is this serving?' and 'Who is this helping?' Determine if the business solves a problem for the greater good of humanity. If the answer is only to make money for yourself, stop and reset your intention.
frameworks.representative_quote: I always ask people what's the why like why are you doing it like what's what is the bigger what can this do for people like what is this solving

frameworks.label: Future-Self Intention Setting
frameworks.summary: A practical exercise for clarifying long-term dreams and goals when starting a new chapter in life.
frameworks.details: 1. Get an envelope, a piece of paper, and a pen. 2. Write your name and a date far in the future (e.g., 18 years from now). 3. Put yourself in that future present moment and write down what you have achieved, where you have gone, and how you have connected with yourself, others, and a higher power. 4. Seal the envelope and open it when you reach that target age.
frameworks.representative_quote: I want you to write a letter to your future selves... put yourself in that present moment and tell yourself where you are. What have you achieved?

mental_models.label: Reversing the Wheel
mental_models.summary: Instead of inventing an entirely new user flow, take a comfortable, established flow and reverse a single key dynamic to change the social outcome.
mental_models.details: Example: Keeping the familiar swipe mechanism of dating apps but reversing the traditional gender dynamic by requiring women to send the first message (The Sadie Hawkins Model).
mental_models.representative_quote: We weren't trying to reinvent the wheel, we were just trying to reverse it.

mental_models.label: Double-Sided Marketplace vs. Content Platform
mental_models.summary: Distinguishing between platforms where scale and endless scrolling drive results (content) versus platforms that require mutual relevance and curated matching (marketplaces).
mental_models.details: In a marketplace, flooding the app with uncurated users ruins the experience. You wouldn't want to meet every single person you pass on a New York City street, so sheer volume is detrimental without relevance.
mental_models.representative_quote: This is not a social network, this is a double-sided marketplace. One person gets on and they have to see someone that is relevant to them.

mental_models.label: Me to We Reframing
mental_models.summary: Shifting the focus of a traumatic event or setback from personal pain to collective benefit.
mental_models.details: Instead of asking how to avenge yourself, you ask how your experience can help others. This transforms a painful exit or public humiliation into the foundational purpose for a new, positive venture.
mental_models.representative_quote: I'm going to take what almost killed me and I'm going to make us stronger.

mental_models.label: The Viral Coin
mental_models.summary: The concept that virality is a neutral mechanism; if toxic behavior can spread virally, positive behavior and kindness can be engineered to do the same.
mental_models.details: Founding a platform on the mission that there is always the other side of the coin to internet toxicity.
mental_models.representative_quote: if toxicity can be viral so can positivity so can kindness

mental_models.label: The Mask of Protection
mental_models.summary: The persona or facade we adopt to shield ourselves from judgment, rejection, or disapproval, which ultimately hides our innate authenticity.
mental_models.details: We are all born deeply authentic, but over time we adopt masks to blend in with the crowd or avoid being disliked, which leads to spiritual bankruptcy.
mental_models.representative_quote: I think we are all inherently deeply authentic people, but then we choose to for whatever reason... put on a mask.

mental_models.label: Ego vs. Soul
mental_models.summary: A lens for evaluating daily actions by imagining your ego on one shoulder and your soul on the other, recognizing that what benefits one usually starves the other.
mental_models.details: Before making a decision, ask yourself if you are doing it for your ego (external validation) or your soul (higher good and inner peace).
mental_models.representative_quote: what's good for your soul is often times not good for your ego and vice versa.

mental_models.label: The Cinderella Constraint
mental_models.summary: Using a strict, non-negotiable time limit to force action and overcome deeply ingrained social hesitations.
mental_models.details: Example: Giving women exactly 24 hours to make the first move on a match before it disappears forever, forcing them to break the societal norm of waiting for the man to speak first.
mental_models.representative_quote: You have 24 hours to make the first move. Otherwise, your carriage turns into a pumpkin.

mental_models.label: Business as a Child / Long-Term Relationship
mental_models.summary: Viewing your company as another child or a long-term relationship to manage guilt and recognize when a break is needed.
mental_models.details: Treating a business like a child eliminates working-mother guilt by reframing it as transitioning between 'children'. Similarly, viewing it as a long-term relationship helps founders recognize when they need a hiatus to cure burnout.
mental_models.representative_quote: I’d been in this long-term relationship with Bumble, and it was time for a break.

mental_models.label: AI as a Dating Concierge
mental_models.summary: Viewing AI not as a synthetic replacement for human connection, but as a personal coach or assistant that helps users overcome insecurities and curate matches.
mental_models.details: Using an AI concierge to talk through insecurities after a breakup, give productive communication tips, or pre-vet matches to save the user from exhaustion.
mental_models.representative_quote: the world’s smartest and most emotionally intelligent matchmaker in existence

heuristics.label: Go online to get offline
heuristics.summary: Design and use technology with the explicit goal of facilitating real-world, physical interactions.

heuristics.label: Revenge is not a plot for success
heuristics.summary: Never build a business or make major life decisions based on a vendetta or a desire to get back at someone.
heuristics.representative_quote: Revenge is not a plot for success. It's not.

heuristics.label: Self-worth does not equal net worth
heuristics.summary: Do not measure your personal value by societal standards of wealth or status.
heuristics.representative_quote: I've come to realize that selfworth does not equal net worth.

heuristics.label: Reverse the wheel, don't reinvent it
heuristics.summary: Look for small, powerful tweaks to existing, comfortable behaviors rather than trying to create entirely new paradigms from scratch.
heuristics.representative_quote: We weren't trying to reinvent the wheel, we were just trying to reverse it.

heuristics.label: Zero tolerance for abuse
heuristics.summary: If a user abuses others or uses hate speech, ban them immediately. Do not compromise on platform safety.
heuristics.representative_quote: If you abuse, you're gone. Zero tolerance for harassment or hate speech.

heuristics.label: Focus on inputs over outputs
heuristics.summary: Prioritize the foundational drivers of your business (like user satisfaction) over lagging metrics (like revenue).
heuristics.representative_quote: A good business focuses on the core inputs that matter most.

heuristics.label: What almost killed me can make us stronger
heuristics.summary: Use your lowest points and personal traumas as the catalyst to build solutions that help others.
heuristics.representative_quote: I'm going to take what almost killed me and I'm going to make us stronger.

heuristics.label: Don't settle for a timeline
heuristics.summary: Never force a relationship just to meet a societal expectation of when you should be married or settled down.
heuristics.representative_quote: Do not date someone if it’s not right... And don’t settle. This settling thing is insane.

signature_quotes.label: On Inputs vs. Outputs
signature_quotes.summary: A good business focuses on the core inputs that matter most.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: A good business does not focus on outputs. A good business focuses on the core inputs that matter most.

signature_quotes.label: On Tech Responsibility
signature_quotes.summary: The minute you encourage someone to use a piece of technology, you are inherently responsible.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: The minute you encourage someone to use a piece of technology, you are inherently responsible.

signature_quotes.label: On Spiritual Bankruptcy
signature_quotes.summary: I will tell you that on paper at my on paper wealthiest according to society standards I was spiritually the poorest I've ever been.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: I will tell you that on paper at my on paper wealthiest according to society standards I was spiritually the poorest I've ever been.

signature_quotes.label: On Reversing the Wheel
signature_quotes.summary: We weren't trying to reinvent the wheel, we were just trying to reverse it.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: We weren't trying to reinvent the wheel, we were just trying to reverse it.

signature_quotes.label: On the Purpose of Connection Tech
signature_quotes.summary: I do not want you to stare at your phone all day. I fail my job if I trap you on the phone.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: I do not want you to stare at your phone all day. I fail my job if I trap you on the phone.

signature_quotes.label: On Contagious Kindness
signature_quotes.summary: Just like bad behavior can become addictive and viral, good behavior can also be contagious and kindness can also be contagious.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Just like bad behavior can become addictive and viral, good behavior can also be contagious and kindness can also be contagious.

signature_quotes.label: On Outdated Dating Dynamics
signature_quotes.summary: For all the advances women had been making in workplaces and corridors of power, the gender dynamics of dating and romance still seemed so outdated.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: For all the advances women had been making in workplaces and corridors of power, the gender dynamics of dating and romance still seemed so outdated.

signature_quotes.label: On Collective Strength
signature_quotes.summary: I'm going to take what almost killed me and I'm going to make us stronger.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: I'm going to take what almost killed me and I'm going to make us stronger.

signature_quotes.label: On Internal Success
signature_quotes.summary: Success is not external. It's just not. It's internal. It really is like no amount of external validation will do it.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: success is not external. It's just not. It's it's internal. It really is like no amount of external validation will do it.

signature_quotes.label: On Social Media Rules
signature_quotes.summary: I don’t know why the leaders of social networks have overlooked the idea of rules.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: I don’t know why the leaders of social networks have overlooked the idea of rules.

anti_patterns.label: Chasing Sheer Growth Over Quality
anti_patterns.summary: Flooding a double-sided marketplace with irrelevant users to boost vanity metrics.
anti_patterns.details: In a matchmaking app, sheer scale degrades the experience. You might get short-term growth, but you eventually lose it because members feel overwhelmed rather than finding meaningful connections.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: When you chase growth, you get it, but then you lose it.

anti_patterns.label: Building Platforms Without Behavioral Guardrails
anti_patterns.summary: Assuming users will behave well without rules, allowing toxic behavior and misogyny to scale unchecked.
anti_patterns.details: It creates a 'wild wild west' where offline toxic mechanics intersect with the dangers of the internet, leading to abuse and a crisis of loneliness.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: I don’t know why the leaders of social networks have overlooked the idea of rules.

anti_patterns.label: Building a Business Out of Revenge
anti_patterns.summary: Operating a business out of a vendetta, revenge, or a chip on your shoulder.
anti_patterns.details: It drains your energy and is a toxic foundation. True success requires pure intention and serving the greater good; revenge is not a viable plot for success.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: Revenge is not a plot for success. It's not.

anti_patterns.label: Optimizing for Screen Time
anti_patterns.summary: Designing connection apps to maximize time spent scrolling rather than facilitating offline meetings.
anti_patterns.details: Dating apps are meant to get you on a date. If a user spends 10 hours a day on a dating app like they do on TikTok, the app is failing its core purpose.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: I do not want you to stare at your phone all day. I fail my job if I trap you on the phone.

anti_patterns.label: Seeking External Validation for Internal Wounds
anti_patterns.summary: Believing that achieving a specific milestone (job, money, relationship) will make you feel 'enough'.
anti_patterns.details: Self-worth does not come from external societal standards or net worth; you can achieve all those things and still be spiritually poor if you haven't done the internal healing.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: success is not external. It's just not. It's it's internal. It really is like no amount of external validation will do it.

anti_patterns.label: Accepting Outdated Gender Dynamics in Romance
anti_patterns.summary: Pushing for equality in the workplace while accepting passive, outdated roles in personal relationships.
anti_patterns.details: It leaves women disempowered in their personal lives, forcing smart, wonderful women to wait around for men to initiate connections.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: Why is there this standard that, as a woman, you can get your dream job but you can't talk to a guy first?

anti_patterns.label: People-Pleasing as a Full-Time Job
anti_patterns.summary: Obsessing over being liked by everyone and caring too much about what others think.
anti_patterns.details: It acts as an unpaid full-time job that physically, spiritually, and mentally bankrupts you, forcing you to wear a mask of inauthenticity.

anti_patterns.label: Using AI to Replace Human Empathy
anti_patterns.summary: Deploying AI bots to act as synthetic replacements for human connection.
anti_patterns.details: People seeking connection want to talk to a real person who has heart and understands human emotions. AI should be used for pattern recognition, sorting, and coaching, not as a replacement for a partner.
